subject: civic
learning_objectives: Explain the importance of selfless activities
Identify people involved in selfless activities in Nigeria

sections.heading: Meaning of Justice
sections.content: Justice is given many definitions by many people. Plato says that a just individual is someone whose soul is guided by a vision of the good. Aristotle says that justice is giving a person his due — when a person receives benefits according to his merit, such is justice. Justice is action according to the requirements of some law. It is fairness. It is action that pays due regard to the proper interests, property and safety of one's fellows.

sections.heading: Importance of Justice
sections.content:
a. Truth: Justice stands for truth. When there is justice, the truth is projected and the right thing is seen to have been done.

b. Peace: Peace is a state of affairs where no direct or physical violence, conflict, war, or quarrelling exists. Justice promotes peace.

c. Divine Command: Justice is a divine law. It emanates from God, and we must uphold these values.

d. Mutual Agreement: Justice is a mutual agreement of people concerned. It is created by the people themselves for their own good and that of the society.

e. Equality: Justice brings equality. It makes everybody equal, with no master-servant relationship. People deserve to be regarded for their effort and productivity and to have their basic needs met.

sections.heading: Meaning of Selflessness
sections.content: Selflessness is defined as having, exhibiting, or being motivated by no concern for oneself. It is the quality of unselfish concern for the welfare of others. Selflessness means we identify with others. We help others because we identify with their problems and their feelings.

sections.heading: Importance of Selfless Activities
sections.content:
a. Conquers Pride: Selfless people act out of motivation to do the right thing and help others. By definition, selflessness cannot involve inflating one's ego.

b. Expands Our Mind: When we act only out of selfish motives, we bind and limit ourselves, calculating how we might benefit from every action. When we act through selfless motives, we do something without serious self-interest. This enables us to get joy from the achievements of others and from serving others.

c. Attractive: Selflessness is attractive. When we are truly selfless, our actions will be appreciated by others.

d. True Friendship: True friendship exists when we render selfless services to people. Real love and friendship must involve a forgetting of self and a willingness to put others first. Selflessness promotes real love and true friendship.

sections.heading: People Involved in Selfless Activities in Nigeria
sections.content:
Many Nigerians have been involved in selfless activities. Notable examples include:
1. Chief Obafemi Awolowo
2. Sir Ahmadu Bello
3. Dr Nnamdi Azikiwe
4. Comrade Mohammed
5. Muritala Mohammed
6. Chief Gani Fawehinmi
